title: STRECKER REACTION
summary: Overview page for the Strecker reaction describing aldehyde or ketone condensation with amines and cyanide to form α-amino nitriles, including classical and asymmetric variants for amino acid synthesis.
notes: Key concepts: aldehyde/ketone + amine + cyanide manifold, α-amino nitrile intermediates, and hydrolysis to α-amino acids under acidic or basic conditions.

title: STRECKER REACTION
summary: Application page for the Strecker reaction highlighting asymmetric α-amino nitrile formation and amino acid synthesis in the preparation of (-)-hemiasterlin, (-)-α-kainic acid, polyoxamic acid lactone, and amiclenomycin.
notes: Examples emphasize chiral imine, zirconium-mediated, sulfinimine-mediated, and Lewis-acid-mediated Strecker variants in natural-product-oriented synthesis.

title: SUZUKI CROSS-COUPLING (SUZUKI-MIYAURA CROSS-COUPLING)
summary: Overview page for the Suzuki cross-coupling describing palladium-catalyzed C-C bond formation between organoboron reagents and organic halides or pseudohalides under basic conditions.
notes: Key concepts: organoboron transmetallation, tolerance of functional groups and water, and broad use for alkenyl, aryl, alkyl, and alkynyl couplings.

title: SUZUKI CROSS-COUPLING (SUZUKI-MIYAURA CROSS-COUPLING)
summary: Application page for the Suzuki cross-coupling featuring TMC-95A, epothilone A, myxalamide A, and oximidine II, showcasing assembly of highly substituted fragments and macrocyclic frameworks.
notes: Examples emphasize B-alkyl Suzuki coupling, alkenylborane/iodoalkene unions, and intramolecular Suzuki-type macrocyclizations using boron-based nucleophiles.

title: SWERN OXIDATION
summary: Overview page for the Swern oxidation describing low-temperature DMSO activation with oxalyl chloride or trifluoroacetic anhydride to convert primary and secondary alcohols into aldehydes and ketones.
notes: Key concepts: activated alkoxysulfonium intermediates, low-temperature operation, and relation to Albright-Goldman, Pfitzner-Moffatt, and Parikh-Doering oxidation manifolds.

title: SWERN OXIDATION
summary: Application page for the Swern oxidation highlighting selective alcohol-to-carbonyl conversions in the synthesis of (+)-deoxyneodolabelline, ircinal A, and (+)-asteltoxin.
notes: Examples emphasize α-hydroxy ketone formation from secondary alcohols, double Swern oxidation to dialdehydes, and HWE olefination sequences enabled by Swern-derived aldehydes.

title: TAKAI-UTIMOTO OLEFINATION (TAKAI REACTION)
summary: Overview page for the Takai-Utimoto olefination describing chromium-mediated conversion of aldehydes into stereodefined alkenyl halides, typically favoring the (E)-isomer.
notes: Key concepts: CrCl2/haloform or gem-dihalide reagents, aldehyde-selective olefination, and formation of (E)-alkenyl iodides, bromides, and chlorides under mild conditions.

title: TAKAI-UTIMOTO OLEFINATION (TAKAI REACTION)
summary: Application page for the Takai-Utimoto olefination showing installation of vinyl halide motifs in aplysiapyranoid C, polycephalin C, bengamide E, and (-)-equisetin.
notes: Examples emphasize highly (E)-selective alkenyl iodide formation, iterative double Takai olefination, and integration with Stille or Suzuki couplings and Diels-Alder strategies.

title: TEBBE OLEFINATION / PETASIS-TEBBE OLEFINATION
summary: Overview page for the Tebbe olefination and Petasis-Tebbe olefination describing titanium-methylene-mediated methylenation of aldehydes, ketones, esters, lactones, amides, and related carbonyl derivatives.
notes: Key concepts: formation of titanocene methylidene from the Tebbe or Petasis reagent, methylenation of carbonyl compounds, and access to enol ethers, enamines, and alkenes.

title: TEBBE OLEFINATION / PETASIS-TEBBE OLEFINATION
summary: Application page for Tebbe/Petasis-Tebbe olefination featuring (+)-epoxydictyene, (+)-laurencin, (+)-21-oxogelsemine/(+)-gelsemine, and methylenated β-lactams and lactams.
notes: Examples emphasize methylenation of lactones, aldehydes, ketones, and electron-deficient amides in complex-molecule synthesis, including cases followed by Claisen rearrangement or downstream olefin functionalization.
